task_completion.description: Navigate to the output or logging section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output', 'Logging', or similarly named tab/section is selected and active within the Options dialog.

task_completion.description: Enable the file output/log to file option
task_completion.visual_evidence: A checkbox for 'log to file', 'file output', or similar logging option is checked/ticked.

task_completion.description: Set the output file path exactly as requested
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text field for the log file path contains exactly 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\ScanLogs\daily_scans.txt'.

task_completion.description: Click OK to apply the settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cursor is seen clicking the 'OK' or 'Apply' button in the Options dialog, or the dialog successfully closes after the correct settings were entered.

integrity.description: Agent used the bcWebCam GUI to configure settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the bcWebCam graphical interface rather than terminal commands or PowerShell scripts modifying configuration files.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual bcWebCam application
integrity.visual_evidence: The window being interacted with clearly belongs to bcWebCam, not a text editor, mock window, or unrelated application.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI by editing config files directly
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no text editors (like Notepad) open editing bcWebCam's internal .ini, .xml, or registry settings directly.
